perturbation.original_text: Intellectual Property Rights means intellectual and industrial property rights, including copyright (including moral rights), patents, know-how, trade secrets, trademarks, service marks, trade names, design rights, registered designs, get-up, database rights, chip topography rights, mask works, utility models, domain names, rights in trade and business names and all similar rights and, in each case: (a) whether registered or not; (b) including any applications to protect or register such rights; (c) including all renewals and extensions of such rights or applications; (d) whether vested, contingent or future; (e) including rights in the nature of unfair competition rights and rights to sue in passing off; and (f) wherever existing;
perturbation.changed_text: Intellectual Property Rights means intellectual and industrial property rights, including copyright (excluding moral rights), patents, know-how, trade secrets, trademarks, service marks, trade names, design rights, registered designs, get-up, database rights, chip topography rights, mask works, utility models, domain names, rights in trade and business names and all similar rights and, in each case: (a) whether registered or not; (b) including any applications to protect or register such rights; (c) including all renewals and extensions of such rights or applications; (d) whether vested, contingent or future; (e) including rights in the nature of unfair competition rights and rights to sue in passing off; and (f) wherever existing;
perturbation.explanation: The initial definition of 'Intellectual Property Rights' includes copyright (including moral rights). By changing this to 'copyright (excluding moral rights)', it directly contradicts the original definition. This affects Section 5 'Intellectual Property Rights', creating ambiguity because the Recipient acknowledges that ownership of the Confidential Information including all Intellectual Property Rights in the Confidential Information remains vested in and shall vest in the Disclosers and its licensors. Now it is unclear whether the moral rights remain vested in the Disclosers and its licensors, since the definition of 'Intellectual Property Rights' exclude moral rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Intellectual Property Rights The Recipient acknowledges that ownership of the Confidential Information including all Intellectual Property Rights in the Confidential Information remains vested in and shall vest in the Disclosers and its licensors, and that no licence or right is granted other than to the extent expressly set out in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of Intellectual Property Rights included 'copyright (including moral rights)'. The changed text modifies this to 'copyright (excluding moral rights)'. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that ownership of all Intellectual Property Rights in the Confidential Information remains vested in the Disclosers and its licensors. The removal of moral rights from the definition introduces ambiguity regarding whether those rights remain vested, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the provided definitions and explanations.

perturbation.original_text: Use means receive, store, transmit, access, read, analyse, disclose, share, print, copy, reproduce, extract, modify, adapt, incorporate, exploit or use Confidential Information in whole or in part in any manner whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: Use means receive, store, transmit, access, read, analyse, disclose, share, print, copy, reproduce, extract, modify, adapt, incorporate, exploit or use Confidential Information in whole or in part in any manner that is publicly accessible.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Use' to 'any manner that is publicly accessible,' it creates ambiguity in Clause 2.1.2. The original definition prohibited any use of Confidential Information except for the Purpose. The revised definition implies that any use is acceptable as long as it's publicly accessible, contradicting the original restriction and potentially undermining the agreement's confidentiality goals. If the Use is only restricted to confidential information in whole or in part in any manner that is publicly accessible, then confidential information is no longer considered to be so.
perturbation.contradicted_text: not Use any Confidential Information in any way except to the extent reasonably necessary for the Purpose, and not Use or benefit from any Confidential Information to procure any commercial advantage over the Disclosers;
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Use' broadly prohibits any use of Confidential Information except for the Purpose, as stated in the contradicted text. The changed definition narrows 'Use' to only apply when the information is publicly accessible. This creates a contradiction because it implies that using confidential information in a non-publicly accessible manner is permissible, conflicting directly with the original restriction and the explicit prohibition in the contradicted text against any unauthorized use ('not Use any Confidential Information in any way except to the extent reasonably necessary for the Purpose'). This makes enforcement unclear as the agreement now provides conflicting guidance on what constitutes permitted use of confidential information.

perturbation.changed_text: Agreement means this agreement excluding schedules, appendices and annexes.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Agreement' is changed to exclude schedules, appendices, and annexes. This creates ambiguity when referencing the 'Agreement' elsewhere. For instance, Section 9.1 states that 'this Agreement and any documents entered into pursuant to it constitute the entire agreement between them.' Because the definition of 'Agreement' now excludes schedules etc, it's unclear if the entire agreement refers to schedules, appendices and annexes, leading to disputes regarding the scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that this Agreement and any documents entered into pursuant to it constitute the entire agreement between them and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and arrangements between them, whether in writing or oral in respect of its subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Agreement' to exclude schedules, appendices, and annexes. The contradicted text states that 'this Agreement and any documents entered into pursuant to it constitute the entire agreement'. The change creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'entire agreement' includes schedules, appendices, and annexes since they are now excluded from the definition of 'Agreement'. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' which occurs when key terms are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: Business Day means a day other than a Saturday, Sunday or bank or public holiday in England or Wales
perturbation.changed_text: Business Day means a day other than a Saturday, Sunday or any holiday observed by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Business Day' is changed from only accounting for 'bank or public holiday in England or Wales' to 'any holiday observed by the Recipient.' This conflicts because the Disclosers are Jasmine Technologies Ltd, a company incorporated in England and Wales, and so any obligation of the Recipient to return any and all materials containing the Confidential Information together with all copies to the Disclosers per the written demand from the Disclosers upon receipt in Section 8.1, has ambiguity of when the Recipient has to follow through with the return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt by the Recipient of a written demand from the Disclosers: 8.1.1 the Recipient must return or procure the return to the Disclosers or, as the Disclosers may require, destroy or procure the destruction of any and all materials containing the Confidential Information together with all copies; 8.1.2 if the Disclosers requires, the Recipient must provide the Disclosers with a certificate or such other evidence as the Disclosers may reasonably require duly signed or executed by an officer of the Recipient confirming that the Recipient has complied with all of its obligations under this Agreement including about return, destruction and deletion of Confidential Information and media; 8.1.3 the Recipient must delete or procure the deletion of all electronic copies of Confidential Information; and 8.1.4 the Recipient must make, and procure that the Authorised Persons shall make, no further Use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Business Day' from specifically referencing 'bank or public holiday in England and Wales' to 'any holiday observed by the Recipient.' This creates an ambiguity and thus a contradiction with Section 8.1.1, which requires the Recipient to return materials upon written demand from Jasmine Technologies Ltd, a company incorporated in England and Wales (Disclosers). The modified definition makes it unclear when the Recipient is obligated to return the materials, as 'any holiday observed by the Recipient' might differ from the holidays observed in England and Wales, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: Authorised Person means, in relation to the Recipient, any of the following only to the extent that they are engaged in respect of the Purpose: its officers, directors, employees, and any other person who has been previously approved in writing by the Disclosers
perturbation.changed_text: Authorised Person means, in relation to the Recipient, any of the following only to the extent that they are engaged in respect of the Purpose: its officers, directors, employees, and any other person whom the Recipient deems necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The change gives the Recipient complete discretion over who is an authorized person, as they can now deem anyone to be an authorized person. This contrasts with the obligation in Section 3.2 that requires Authorised Persons to comply with confidentiality obligations. Now, the definition of an 'Authorised person' is up to the discretion of the recipient and are no longer required to enter into a confidentiality agreement directly with the Disclosers in reasonably similar terms as those set out under this Agreement as stated in section 3.2, if required by the Disclosers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient further undertakes to procure that all Authorised Persons to whom it discloses the Confidential Information shall comply with the confidentiality obligations under this Agreement and, if required by the Disclosers, to procure that they enter into a confidentiality agreement directly with the Disclosers in reasonably similar terms as those set out under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Recipient to unilaterally define 'Authorised Person,' removing the Disclosers' control over who is bound by confidentiality agreements. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Recipient to ensure that 'Authorised Persons' comply with confidentiality obligations and potentially enter into direct agreements with the Disclosers. The change creates an ambiguity and undermines the Disclosers' ability to enforce confidentiality, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.'
